title: Read/write pattern model
description: Cache-aside, read/write-through, and write-behind differ in who owns loads, writes, and durability.
body: Use when reasoning about who owns reads and writes between cache and store. Cache-aside: the app reads cache, loads from store on miss and populates, and invalidates the key on write. Read-through/write-through: the cache library loads and persists synchronously on the app's behalf. Write-behind: writes hit the cache and flush to the store asynchronously. Do not conflate them — each carries a different failure and consistency profile. Actions: pick by durability and latency needs. Validation signals include a documented staleness and durability bound. Risk boundary: write-behind can lose unflushed writes on a crash.
